heading: Smart grid interoperability framework
label: 17385 Smart grid interoperability framework
notes: Codification, referred to in subsec. (c), was in the original “enactment” and was translated as meaning the date of enactment of , to reflect the probable intent of Congress.December 19, 2007Pub. L. 110–140
Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.text: the Director shall seek input and cooperation from the Commission, OEDER and its Smart Grid Task Force, the Smart Grid Advisory Committee, other relevant Federal and State agencies; and

the Director shall also solicit input and cooperation from private entities interested in such protocols and standards, including but not limited to the Gridwise Architecture Council, the International Electrical and Electronics Engineers, the National Electric Reliability Organization recognized by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, and National Electrical Manufacturer’s Association.

to accommodate traditional, centralized generation and transmission resources and consumer distributed resources, including distributed generation, renewable generation, energy storage, energy efficiency, and demand response and enabling devices and systems;

children.label: A
children.text: load reduction to reduce total electrical demand;

children.label: B
children.text: adjustment of load to provide grid ancillary services; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the event of a reliability crisis that threatens an outage, short-term load shedding to help preserve the stability of the grid; and
such voluntary standards should incorporate appropriate manufacturer lead time.1So in original. Does not fit with subsec. (b) introductory provisions.1

children.heading: Timing of framework development
children.label: c Timing of framework development
children.text: The Institute shall begin work pursuant to this section within 60 days of . The Institute shall provide and publish an initial report on progress toward recommended or consensus standards and protocols within 1 year after , further reports at such times as developments warrant in the judgment of the Institute, and a final report when the Institute determines that the work is completed or that a Federal role is no longer necessary.December 19, 2007December 19, 2007

children.heading: Standards for interoperability in Federal jurisdiction
children.label: d Standards for interoperability in Federal jurisdiction
children.text: At any time after the Institute’s work has led to sufficient consensus in the Commission’s judgment, the Commission shall institute a rulemaking proceeding to adopt such standards and protocols as may be necessary to insure smart-grid functionality and interoperability in interstate transmission of electric power, and regional and wholesale electricity markets.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: e Authorization
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for the purposes of this section $5,000,000 to the Institute to support the activities required by this subsection for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.2So in original. Probably should be “section”.2
